Why India may be taking risky bet with $500 billion US dealMarco Rubio said in New Delhi that India had committed to buying $500 billion of American goods over five years. The remark has intensified scrutiny of whether the proposed arrangement offers India enough return.

But banks are quietly noticing something interesting: women borrowers are often proving to be more reliable when it comes to repaying loans. So, are lenders beginning to trust women more with credit?

CBI creates 'tunnel view' to reconstruct Twisha Sharma's final hours on May 12The 'tunnel view' technique is the method through which investigators synchronise CCTV, phone records, Wi-Fi logs, internet activity, smart-device data, forensic mapping, and witness accounts into a focused timeline to digitally reconstruct a crime's final moments.

Live: Rahul Gandhi-Siddaramaiah meet underway, Rajya Sabha offer likely on agendaKarnataka CM News Live Updates: The Congress party on Thursday described Siddaramaiah's decision to step down as Chief Minister as a 'selfless' move aimed at enabling a leadership transition in Karnataka. Senior leaders amplified images of him embracing his deputy DK Shivakumar, underscoring a display of unity at the top of the state leadership. Soon after tendering his resignation, Siddaramaiah left for Delhi along with his son Yathindra, party national general secretary Randeep Singh Surjewala, and others to meet the party high command and discuss the next phase of the transition. Party sources said both Siddaramaiah and Shivakumar travelled separately to Delhi after the resignation exercise, amid intensified consultations over Karnataka's leadership change. In Delhi, discussions are expected to focus on selecting the new Congress Legislature Party leader, the composition of the Cabinet, and broader organisational adjustments. These may also include deliberations over the post of Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee president, currently held by Shivakumar. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all live updates.
